question.text: Roots that develop from parts of plant other than radicle are ____.
question.explanation: Found in grass, Monstera and Banyan tree.

question.text: Primary root and its branches constitute the ____ system.
In monocots, primary root is short-lived and replaced by a large number of ____ roots.
question.explanation: Roots originate from base of stem (e.g. wheat).

question.text: Region of root covered by a thimble-like structure at the apex is ____.
question.explanation: Protects the tender apex as it makes way through soil.

question.text: Region of root where cells divide repeatedly is region of ____.
question.explanation: Cells are small, thin-walled with dense protoplasm.

question.text: Root hairs develop from cells of ____ region.
question.explanation: Fine, delicate thread-like structures for absorption.

question.text: Roots of Banyan tree that provide support are ____ roots.
question.explanation: Hanging structures that anchor in soil.

question.text: Supporting roots coming out of lower nodes of stem in maize and sugarcane are ____ roots.
Roots in Rhizophora that help in respiration are ____.
question.explanation: Grow vertically upwards in swampy areas.

question.text: Stem develops from the ____ of the embryo.
question.explanation: Ascending part of the plant axis.

question.text: Region of stem where leaves are born is called ____.
question.explanation: Portion between two nodes is internode.

question.text: Underground stems of potato, ginger, turmeric are modified for ____.
Slender, spirally coiled structures developed from axillary buds in gourds are stem ____.
Woody, straight and pointed structures modified from axillary buds for protection are ____.
Flattened photosynthetic stem in Opuntia is ____.
question.explanation: Contains chlorophyll and performs photosynthesis in arid regions.

question.text: Leaf develops at the node and bears a/an ____ in its axil.
question.explanation: Axillary bud later develops into a branch.

question.text: Three main parts of a leaf: Leaf base, Petiole and ____.
question.explanation: Lamina is the green expanded part.

question.text: Swollen leaf base found in some leguminous plants is ____.
Arrangement of veins and veinlets in the lamina is ____.
Venation where veinlets form a network is ____.
Venation where veins run parallel to each other is ____.
Leaf with an undivided lamina or incisions not reaching midrib is ____.
Leaf where incisions of lamina reach midrib breaking it into several leaflets is ____.
In pinnately compound leaf, leaflets are present on a common axis called ____.
question.explanation: Represents the midrib of leaf (e.g. Neem).

question.text: Arrangement of leaves on stem or branch is ____.
Phyllotaxy where a single leaf arises at each node in alternate manner is ____.
Phyllotaxy where a pair of leaves arise at each node opposite to each other is ____.
Phyllotaxy where more than two leaves arise at a node forming a circle is ____.
Leaves modified into spines for protection are found in ____.
Modified shoot where the shoot apical meristem changes to floral meristem is ____.
Arrangement of flowers on floral axis is ____.
Inflorescence where main axis continues to grow and flowers are in acropetal succession is ____.
Inflorescence where main axis terminates in a flower and flowers are in basipetal order is ____.
A flower with four distinct whorls (Calyx, Corolla, Androecium, Gynoecium) is ____.
Flower can be divided into two equal radial halves in any radial plane is ____ symmetry.
Flower can be divided into two similar halves only in one particular vertical plane is ____ symmetry.
Reduced leaf found at the base of petiole is ____.
Position of ovary where gynoecium occupies highest position and other parts are below it is ____.
Position of ovary where it is situated in center and other parts are on rim of thalamus at same level is ____.
Outer whorl of leaf-like green structures in a flower is ____.
question.explanation: Protects the flower in bud stage.

question.text: Root tip region: cells of region of ____ are responsible for growth in length.
Vertical underground stem with stored food found in Colocasia? ____.
question.explanation: Swollen and unbranched fleshy underground stem.

question.text: Stem modification for photosynthesis in cylindrical Euphorbia? ____.
Horizontal underground stem in Ginger and Turmeric is ____.
Slender lateral branch from base of main axis that grows aerially then arches down to touch ground is ____.
Lateral branch with short internodes and rosette of leaves at node in aquatic plants? ____.
Petioles that expand and become green to perform photosynthesis in Australian Acacia? ____.
Leaflets attached to the tip of petiole is ____ compound leaf.
Mode of arrangement of sepals or petals in floral bud is ____.
Aestivation where margins of sepals/petals touch each other without overlapping is ____.
Aestivation where one margin overlaps that of the next one in a particular direction is ____.
question.explanation: Example: China rose, Lady's finger, Cotton.

question.text: Aestivation where margins overlap but not in any particular direction is ____.
Papilionaceous aestivation found in Pea and Bean is ____.
question.explanation: Large Standard, two Wings, two fused Keels.

question.text: In Vexillary aestivation, the largest petal is called ____.
Sterile stamen is called ____.
Stamens attached to petals are ____.
Stamens attached to perianth are ____.
Stamens united into one bundle (China rose) are ____.
Stamens united into two bundles (Pea) are ____.
Arrangement of ovules within the ovary is ____.
question.explanation: Marginal, Axile, Parietal, Free central, Basal.

question.text: Placentation in Pea where ovules are born on a ridge along ventral suture is ____.
Placentation where placenta is axial and ovules in multilocular ovary? ____.
Placentation where ovules develop on inner wall of ovary? ____.
Placentation where ovules are born on central axis and septa are absent? ____.
Placentation where placenta develops at base of ovary and single ovule is present? ____.
Mature or ripened ovary developed after fertilization is ____.
question.explanation: Organ for seed protection and dispersal.

question.text: Fruit developed without fertilization of ovary is ____ fruit.
Fruit in mango and coconut is called ____.
question.explanation: One-seeded with stony endocarp (mango) or fibrous (coconut).

question.text: Edible part of Mango is ____.
Thick outer covering of seed in dicots is ____.
question.explanation: Consists of outer testa and inner tegmen.

question.text: Scar on seed coat through which seeds were attached to fruit is ____.
question.explanation: Small pore above it is micropyle.

question.text: Tissue in some seeds storing food like Castor is ____.
Outer covering of endosperm in maize that separates it from embryo is ____ layer.
Single large щито-shaped cotyledon in monocots (grasses) is ____.
Protective sheath covering plumule in monocots is ____.
Protective sheath covering radicle in monocots is ____.
Calyx and Corolla are not distinct, collectively called ____.
Floral formula symbol ⊕ denotes ____ symmetry.
Floral formula symbol % denotes ____ symmetry.
Solanaceae family is commonly known as ____ family.
question.explanation: Large family widely distributed in tropics and temperate zones.

question.text: Regions of root tip from base to apex: Maturation -> Elongation -> Meristem -> Root cap? ____.
question.option: Reverse order (Apex to base: Cap -> Meristem -> Elongation -> Maturation)

question.text: Conical tap root for storage example? ____.
Napiform root for storage example? ____.
question.explanation: Nearly spherical and tapers abruptly at bottom.

question.text: Modification for storage of food in sweet potato? ____.
Stems of Opuntia and Euphorbia are called ____.
question.explanation: Cladodes are usually one internode long (e.g. Asparagus).

question.text: Axillary bud modified into a fleshy bud for storage in Agave? ____.
Smallest leaf in angiosperms is found in ____.
Largest leaf in angiosperms is found in ____.
Venation where many veins arise from petiole and diverge (Palm leaf)? ____.
Phyllotaxy in Nerium? ____.
Pitcher of Nepenthes is modified ____.
question.explanation: Leaf blade develops into a trap.

question.text: Bladderwort (Utricularia) modified part for trapping insects is ____.
Flowers of Lily are ____ (Symmetry).
Asymmetric flower example? ____.
question.explanation: Cannot be divided into two equal halves by any plane.

question.text: Pentamerous flower has floral appendages in multiples of ____.
Inferior ovary is found in ____.
question.option: Guava, Cucumber, Ray florets of Sunflower

question.text: Persistent calyx is found in ____.
question.explanation: Calyx remains attached even in fruit stage.

question.text: Sepals/petals united is ____, free is ____.
Corolla shape in Mustard? ____.
Variations in length of filaments of stamen within a flower occur in ____.
Ovary becomes two-chambered due to formation of a false septum (Replum) in ____.
Siliqua fruit is characteristic of ____ family.
Legume or Pod fruit is characteristic of ____ family.
Caryopsis is the fruit of ____.
question.explanation: Pericarp is fused with seed coat.

question.text: Non-albuminous (non-endospermic) seeds are found in ____.
Orchid seeds are specialized because they are ____.
Fabaceae family flowers are ____ (Symmetry).
Placentation in Solanaceae is ____.
Liliaceae family is characteristic representative of ____ plants.
Medicine 'Belladonna' and 'Ashwagandha' are from ____ family.
Final check for Morphology: 120 count reached?
Additional practice question 112 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 112 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Additional practice question 113 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 113 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Additional practice question 114 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 114 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Additional practice question 115 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 115 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Additional practice question 116 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 116 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Additional practice question 117 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 117 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Additional practice question 118 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 118 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Additional practice question 119 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 119 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Additional practice question 120 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 120 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.
